The state of having complete legal control of something; possession; proprietorship.
A body of one or more persons convened for the accomplishment of some specific purpose, typically with formal protocols.
That branch of government which is responsible for making, or having the power to make, a law or laws.
One who researches.
Someone who or something that trails.
A large building that is fortified and contains many defences; in previous ages often inhabited by a nobleman or king.
An outdoor area containing one or more types of plants, usually plants grown for food or ornamental purposes.

vocab.meaning: To fail to hit.
An unsubscription.
A grotesque representation of a figure; a gargoyle.
A small ticket or sign giving information about something to which it is attached or intended to be attached.

vocab.meaning: To wish, desire.
Any compound having a specified (range of) molecular weight(s)
To do something.

vocab.meaning: The part of the body of an animal or human which contains the brain, mouth and main sense organs.
vocab.example: Be careful when you pet that dog on the head; it may bite.

vocab.meaning: To keep (something) while not in use, generally in a place meant for that purpose.
vocab.example: I'll store these books in the attic.

vocab.meaning: A form of rhetoric in which the writer or speaker uses logic as the main argument.
The place where one lives; one's home.
A lawyer; one who advises or represents others in legal matters as a profession.
A grotesque representation of a figure; a gargoyle.
A measure of the mass of matter contained by a unit volume.
A hundred-dollar bill, or any other note denominated 100 (e.g. a hundred euros).
One who operates.
Vagina
Able to be sustained.
Of or pertaining to statistics.
A speaking or notice of anything, usually in a brief or cursory manner. Used especially in the phrase make mention of.
The act of innovating; the introduction of something new, in customs, rites, etc.
A person, firm or other entity which pays for or hires the services of another person.
One of a set of parallel lines.
To make better; improve.
To perform a work or labour; to exert power or strength, physical or mechanical; to act.

Any of various bladed or pointed hand weapons, originally designating an Anglo-Saxon sword, and later a weapon of infantry, especially in the 14th and 15th centuries, commonly consisting of a broad, heavy, double-edged, hook-shaped blade, with a short pike at the back and another at the top, attached to the end of a long staff.
A room containing a shower and/or bathtub, and (typically but not necessarily) a toilet.
A building, wing or dependency set apart and adapted for lodging and feeding (and training) animals with hoofs, especially horses.
vocab.example: There were stalls for fourteen horses in the squire's stables.

vocab.meaning: A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.
A statement of the meaning of a word or word group or a sign or symbol (dictionary definitions).

A physician; a member of the medical profession; one who is trained and licensed to heal the sick or injured. The final examination and qualification may award a doctor degree in which case the post-nominal letters are D.O., DPM, M.D., DMD, DDS, in the US or MBBS in the UK.
vocab.example: If you still feel unwell tomorrow, see your doctor.

vocab.meaning: A section of learning or teaching into which a wider learning content is divided.
vocab.example: In our school a typical working week consists of around twenty lessons and ten hours of related laboratory work.

vocab.meaning: A movie theatre, a movie house
vocab.example: The cinema is right across the street from the restaurant.

vocab.meaning: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.
vocab.example: These shares are a valuable asset.

vocab.meaning: A process of choosing a leader, members of parliament, councillors or other representatives by popular vote.
vocab.example: How did you vote in (UK also: at) the last election?

vocab.meaning: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
vocab.example: He drank the water I gave him.

vocab.meaning: An action or statement in response to a stimulus or other event.
vocab.example: The announcement of the verdict brought a violent reaction.

To lift, raise up.
To give a title to.
Very bad or intense.
To bring into being; give rise to.
Short for stainless steel.
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
Very fine in quality or luxurious.
The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.

vocab.meaning: Someone who watches over something; a person in charge of something or someone.
vocab.example: The camp monitors look after the children during the night, when the teachers are asleep.

vocab.meaning: The act of excepting or excluding; exclusion; restriction by taking out something which would otherwise be included, as in a class, statement, rule.
To be alive; to have life.
vocab.example: He's not expected to live for more than a few months.

vocab.meaning: An amount of time or a particular time interval.
In a successful manner; with success; without failing.
In conformance to, or in agreement with.
An edifice or building.
A single act of visiting.
(focus) Of a primary or central nature, first and foremost
A geographic area owned or controlled by a single person or organization.

vocab.meaning: The power or ability to generate an outcome
A difference in lightness, brightness and/or hue between two colours that makes them more or less distinguishable.
An act of recommending.
To hit a fly ball; to hit a fly ball that is caught for an out. Compare ground (verb) and line (verb).

vocab.meaning: The process or art of finding candidates for a post in an organization, or recruits for the armed forces.

The act of adopting.
To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).

vocab.meaning: Given to expending a lot of money; profligate, lavish.
To intend.
An act of capturing; a seizing by force or stratagem.
A unit of mass equal to 16 avoirdupois ounces (= 453.592 37 g). Today this value is the most common meaning of "pound" as a unit of weight.
Any of the Old World mammals of the family Bovidae, such as the Cape buffalo, Syncerus caffer, or the water buffalo Bubalus bubalis.
The quality of being organized.

vocab.meaning: A level or flat surface.
To make plain, manifest, or intelligible; to clear of obscurity; to illustrate the meaning of.
vocab.example: To explain a chapter of the Bible.

vocab.meaning: A set of structured activities.
vocab.example: Our program for today’s exercise class includes swimming and jogging.

vocab.meaning: Someone or something wished for.
vocab.example: It is my desire to speak with you.

vocab.meaning: Great skill or knowledge in a particular field or hobby.
vocab.example: The scientist has expertise in the field of nuclear fusion.

vocab.meaning: (within a machine or machinery) Any mechanical means for the conversion or control of motion, or the transmission or control of power.
To live in a tent or similar temporary accommodation.
vocab.example: We're planning to camp in the field until Sunday.

vocab.meaning: Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
vocab.example: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.

vocab.meaning: A sports competition, especially for track and field (a track meet) or swimming (a swim meet).
Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.
(heading) To capture, overtake.
In the end; at some later time, especially after a long time, a series of problems, struggles, delays or setbacks.
Having a visible or identifying mark.
To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.
vocab.example: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.

vocab.meaning: To ascertain the quantity of a unit of material via calculated comparison with respect to a standard.
vocab.example: We measured the temperature with a thermometer.   You should measure the angle with a spirit level.

vocab.meaning: A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
vocab.example: Beer is often sold in bottles.

vocab.meaning: An understanding between entities to follow a specific course of conduct.

vocab.meaning: To think about seriously.

vocab.meaning: Characterized by the creation of new ideas or inventions.
The action of rubbing, kneading or hitting someone's body, to help the person relax, prepare for muscular action (as in contact sports) or to relieve aches.

vocab.meaning: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.
The end, finish, close or last part of something.

A numerical value equal to 1,000 = 10 × 100 = 103

vocab.meaning: An unrealistic story depicting past events.

vocab.meaning: A type of large constricting snake.
A terrifying and dangerous creature.

A solid upright structure designed usually to support a larger structure above it, such as a roof or horizontal beam, but sometimes for decoration.

A self-paced learning exercise; a lesson prepared so that a student can learn at their own speed, at their convenience.
